title: Implement shader-based vertex displacement
summary: Enhance existing vertex shaders with proper 3D noise-based displacement so audio reactivity produces sculptural, volumetric deformation rather than flat oscillation. The codebase already has ShaderMaterial with bass/treble uniforms and per-vertex randomness — this ticket layers richer displacement functions on top.

currentState.shaderInfrastructure: ShaderMaterial with 20+ uniforms, bass/treble energy, per-vertex aRandom attribute already in place
currentState.existingDisplacement: Radial expansion, Y-axis twist, directional drift, treble jitter, breathing scale, pointer repulsion — all GPU-side
currentState.noiseFunction: Simple sine-based layeredNoise() with 1-3 octaves — not true 3D spatial noise
currentState.gap: Current displacement feels oscillatory/2D because it uses sin/cos waves rather than coherent 3D noise fields. No wave propagation, no sculptural morphing, no displacement that respects volumetric structure.

steps.title: Add 3D simplex noise GLSL function
steps.description: Create a shared GLSL include with a proper 3D simplex noise function (Ashima/webgl-noise style) that both point cloud and ribbon shaders can use. This replaces the sine-based layeredNoise() with spatially coherent 3D noise that produces organic, sculptural displacement patterns.
details: Implement snoise(vec3) — classic 3D simplex noise returning -1 to 1
Implement fbm(vec3, octaves) — fractal Brownian motion layering multiple noise octaves
Keep it self-contained so it can be prepended to both vertex shaders
Use permutation-based approach (no texture lookups) for compatibility

steps.title: Refactor vertex shaders to use 3D noise displacement
steps.description: Replace the sine-based displacement in pointWarp.vert.glsl and ribbonWarp.vert.glsl with 3D simplex noise-driven displacement. Bass drives large-scale low-frequency noise deformation, treble drives high-frequency fine-grain noise displacement.
details: Bass macro displacement: use fbm(position * lowFreq + time * cadence, 2) scaled by uBassEnergy to push vertices along their radial direction — produces large sculptural warping
Treble micro displacement: use snoise(position * highFreq + time * fastRate) on each axis independently, scaled by uTrebleEnergy — produces shimmering surface detail
Temporal evolution: slowly animate the noise sampling coordinate (uTime * 0.05) so the displacement field drifts and morphs over 20-60 second cycles
Preserve existing pointer repulsion and breathing scale layers
Replace layeredNoise() calls with the new noise3d functions
Add new uniforms: uDisplacementScale (overall amplitude), uNoiseEvolutionRate (temporal drift speed)

steps.title: Add displacement uniforms and wire them through the update pipeline
steps.description: Add the new displacement-related uniforms to the geometry system creation and frame update paths so they respond to audio and evolve over time.
details: Add uDisplacementScale uniform — driven by motionAmplitude (respects prefers-reduced-motion)
Add uNoiseEvolutionRate uniform — driven by cadence mapping (time-of-day rhythm)
Ensure bass/treble uniform updates use the existing smoothed values from FrameState
Prepend noise3d.glsl source to vertex shader strings at material creation time
No new CPU-side per-point loops — all displacement remains GPU-side

steps.title: Tune displacement parameters for sculptural feel
steps.description: Adjust noise frequencies, displacement amplitudes, and temporal rates so the deformation feels organic and volumetric rather than jittery or flat.
details: Bass displacement frequency: ~0.3-0.8 (large spatial wavelength for sculptural warps)
Bass displacement amplitude: ~0.15-0.4 world units at full energy
Treble displacement frequency: ~2.0-4.0 (fine spatial detail)
Treble displacement amplitude: ~0.02-0.08 world units (subtle shimmer)
Evolution rate: noise coordinate drifts ~0.03-0.08 units/second for slow morphing
Ensure reduced-motion mode scales displacement amplitude to 20% via uMotionAmplitude
Verify displacement looks volumetric from multiple camera angles (depth/parallax visible)

steps.title: Update VisualParams and mappings for new displacement controls
steps.description: Extend the VisualParams type and mapSignalsToVisuals to include displacement scale and evolution rate, seeded from session context.
details: Add displacementScale to VisualParams — derived from structureComplexity and motionAmplitude
Add noiseEvolutionRate to VisualParams — derived from cadence with seeded variation
Ensure evolveParams() modulates these over time for non-static behavior
Keep mappings indirect/partially-legible per project rules

steps.title: Verify build, test, and definition of done
steps.description: Run production build, verify no console errors, confirm all acceptance criteria and definition-of-done requirements are met.
details: npm run build passes with no errors
No console errors on normal load
No forbidden storage APIs used
Scene works with and without successful autoplay
Mute toggle and info overlay still work
Displacement is visible and sculptural in the 3D scene
Bass produces large-scale warping, treble produces fine shimmer
Deformation visibly evolves over 30+ seconds (not static)

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.AC1: Geometry deformation occurs in a ShaderMaterial
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.AC2: Bass energy affects large-scale displacement in 3D
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.AC3: Treble energy affects fine-grain displacement or point size
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.AC4: Deformation evolves over time (not static modulation)

risksAndMitigations.risk: Simplex noise computation cost on lower-end GPUs
risksAndMitigations.mitigation: Use 2-octave fbm max for bass, single snoise call for treble; reduced-motion mode can lower octave count

risksAndMitigations.risk: Displacement amplitude too large causes points to scatter incoherently
risksAndMitigations.mitigation: Displace along radial direction (normalize(position)) rather than arbitrary axes for bass — preserves volumetric structure

risksAndMitigations.risk: Noise3d.glsl string concatenation breaks shader compilation
risksAndMitigations.mitigation: Test shader compilation in both geometry systems immediately after wiring; use raw string import via Vite

nonGoals: No new geometry systems or modes — enhance existing point cloud and ribbon shaders
No normal-mapped surfaces — remain point-cloud based
